food_subgroup: Roti prata with egg incorporated before cooking — egg broken onto the stretched dough sheet before folding and pan-frying on tawa with ghee; served with dhal curry

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has paratha as a category (FOODON_03301710). Egg prata is a specific stuffed variant of layered South Asian flatbread; no exact FoodOn term exists for Singapore egg roti prata with dhal curry.
ontology.foodex2_description: Singapore egg roti prata — layered wheat flatbread with egg incorporated, cooked on tawa, served with dhal curry
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: A0EKF is the closest FoodEx2 parent for flatbreads/paratha. No exact FoodEx2 code exists for Singapore egg roti prata with dhal curry.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for Singapore egg roti prata. SNOMED CT food hierarchy does not extend to this level of regional specificity.

serving.description: 1 piece egg prata (120g) with 50g dhal curry = 170g total
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is one piece of egg prata (approximately 120g) served with 50g of dhal curry, totalling 170g — the typical portion at a Singapore mamak or kopitiam stall.

nutrient_source.title: Roti Prata Egg — Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) Foodo Database / ICMR-NIN Indian Food Composition Tables
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) / Indian Council of Medical Research — National Institute of Nutrition (ICMR-NIN)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Roti Prata Egg, cross-referenced with ICMR-NIN Indian Food Composition Tables for egg-enriched flatbread. Values represent the composite dish (prata dough with egg filling) per 100g edible portion. Dhal curry contribution is reflected in the per-serving GL calculation.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Roti Prata (Egg) — University of Sydney GI Database; egg prata GI ~75–80, slightly lower than plain prata (~82) due to egg protein moderating glycaemic response
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI value of 77 is referenced from the University of Sydney GI Database for egg prata / egg paratha. Egg prata GI is reported in the range ~75–80, marginally lower than plain prata (~82) due to the egg protein content providing modest glycaemic buffering. GL per serving calculated as: GI (77) × carbohydrate per serving (40.2g from 120g prata portion) / 100 = 30.9. Confidence 0.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content 278mg/100g; a standard 170g serving (120g prata + 50g dhal) delivers approximately 473mg sodium
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should limit egg prata to one piece per meal and request reduced-salt dhal; the combined sodium from prata and dhal curry approaches one-third of the 1500mg daily sodium target recommended for hypertensive individuals.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (~77) and very high glycemic load per serving (30.9) from refined wheat flour base
type2_diabetes.guidance: Patients with type 2 diabetes should strongly limit egg prata consumption — the GI of ~77 and GL of 30.9 per serving will cause a significant postprandial glucose spike; if consumed, limit to one piece, eat dhal curry first to slow gastric emptying, and pair with additional protein to partially buffer the glycaemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 6.8g/100g (from ghee) and cholesterol 115mg/100g (from egg); a 120g prata portion delivers ~8.2g saturated fat and ~138mg dietary cholesterol
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should limit egg prata to occasional consumption — the ghee-derived saturated fat (6.8g/100g) and egg-derived cholesterol (115mg/100g) contribute meaningfully to saturated fat and dietary cholesterol intake; the trans fat content (0.1g/100g) is low but present, and the high-GI refined flour base promotes postprandial triglyceride elevation.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High GI promotes systemic inflammation; egg provides beneficial micronutrients (vitamin D 0.8mcg, selenium 18mcg, folate 32mcg, vitamin B12 0.5mcg per 100g) relevant to RA management
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Egg prata has a mixed profile for rheumatoid arthritis — the egg enrichment provides clinically relevant anti-inflammatory micronutrients (vitamin D, selenium, folate, B12), but the high-GI refined flour base promotes inflammatory pathways; consume in moderation and prioritise the dhal curry for its anti-inflammatory legume content.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Egg Prata (Roti Prata Telur) is the most popular prata variant in Singapore and a staple of mamak (Indian Muslim) stalls and kopitiams. It is made by stretching wheat flour dough into a thin, almost translucent sheet on a greased tawa, cracking one or two eggs directly onto the dough surface, folding the dough over and around the egg to enclose it, then pan-frying the parcel in ghee until the outside is golden and flaky while the inside remains soft and moist with steamed egg. The result is a layered, flaky flatbread with pockets of soft-set egg between the wheat layers — richer, chewier, and more substantial than plain prata. It is always served with dhal curry (yellow lentil soup spiced with cumin, turmeric, and coriander) for dipping. Nutritionally, egg prata delivers 295 kcal/100g with 10.5g protein, 14.8g fat (6.8g saturated from ghee), and 33.5g carbohydrate. The egg addition contributes vitamin D (0.8mcg/100g), selenium (18mcg/100g), folate (32mcg/100g), vitamin B12 (0.5mcg/100g), and cholesterol (115mg/100g). The glycemic index is high (~77) and the glycemic load per standard serving (120g prata + 50g dhal) is 30.9 — making it a significant glycaemic challenge for patients with type 2 diabetes. The dish is vegetarian and halal. It is most commonly eaten at breakfast or supper at 24-hour prata stalls.
common_user_queries: egg prata vs plain prata calories

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database (primary approved source for Singapore dishes) cross-referenced with ICMR-NIN Indian Food Composition Tables. GI value referenced from University of Sydney GI Database for egg prata in the range 75–80. All mandatory multilingual names present. Ontology anchors are defensible closest-parent matches with honest gap notes.
notes: vitamin_c_mg is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo — egg prata contains no vitamin C as neither wheat flour, ghee, nor egg contribute meaningful vitamin C. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation data for Singapore egg roti prata not available in HPB Foodo or ICMR-NIN tables as of 2026-04. water_g set to -1: moisture content for this composite dish not reported in the primary sources consulted. trans_fat_g reported as 0.1g/100g from ghee contribution (trace level confirmed by HPB). GL per serving calculated as GI (77) × available carbohydrate from 120g prata portion (40.2g) / 100 = 30.9. The 170g default serving (120g prata + 50g dhal) is the standard mamak stall portion per HPB Foodo. A duplicate '1 plate / 400g' portion entry was present in the input; only the verified 170g default serving is retained as primary. Sodium per 170g serving ≈ 473mg (prata 278mg/100g × 1.2 + dhal contribution estimated).
